REPUBLIC OF KENYA
IN THE HIGH COURT OF KENYA AT NAIROBI
CIVIL CASE NO. 142 OF 2024

BETWEEN

JANE ACHIENG OTIENO                              PETITIONER

AND

SUNRISE MICROFINANCE LIMITED                     RESPONDENT

JUDGMENT

CORAM: HON. JUSTICE R.M. KARIUKI

BACKGROUND
This matter came before this court by way of a petition filed on 14th February 2024.
The Petitioner seeks declarations that the Respondent's debt collection practices
violated her constitutional rights under Articles 28 and 31 of the Constitution of
Kenya, 2010. The Petitioner avers that the Respondent contacted her employer and
relatives without her consent and disclosed her personal financial information to
third parties.

FACTS
The Petitioner entered into a digital loan agreement with the Respondent on 3rd January
2024 for a sum of KES 15,000, repayable within 30 days. Upon default, the Respondent's
recovery agents contacted the Petitioner's employer on five separate occasions between
5th and 12th February 2024, disclosing the existence and amount of the debt.

ISSUES FOR DETERMINATION
1. Whether the Respondent's debt collection practices violated the Petitioner's right
   to privacy under Article 31 of the Constitution of Kenya, 2010.
2. Whether the disclosure of the Petitioner's financial information to her employer
   was lawful under the Data Protection Act, 2019.
3. Whether the Petitioner is entitled to the remedies sought.

ANALYSIS
On the first issue, Article 31 of the Constitution guarantees every person the right
to privacy, which includes the right not to have information relating to their family
or private affairs unnecessarily required or revealed. This court finds that a person's
financial indebtedness falls squarely within the category of private affairs protected
by this provision.

FINDINGS
This court finds as follows: The Respondent violated the Petitioner's right to privacy
under Article 31 of the Constitution of Kenya, 2010. The Respondent's processing of
the Petitioner's contact data for debt collection purposes violated the Data Protection
Act, 2019. The Respondent's conduct constituted harassment under the Consumer Protection
Act, 2012.

ORDER
Accordingly, this court makes the following orders:
1. A declaration that the Respondent violated the Petitioner's constitutional right to privacy.
2. The Respondent shall pay the Petitioner general damages of KES 200,000.
3. The Respondent shall cease and desist from contacting third parties in connection
   with the Petitioner's loan account.
4. The Respondent shall pay costs of this petition.

COSTS
Costs are awarded to the Petitioner to be taxed if not agreed.

Dated and delivered at Nairobi this 28th day of March 2024.

R.M. KARIUKI
JUDGE
HIGH COURT OF KENYA
